Title: Select Shipping Address â€” Full Happy-Path Journey (B2B org user)
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:TECHFLOW_ADMIN]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
1. User is authenticated as B2B org member with org that has â‰¥2 active addresses seeded across â‰¥2 countries.
2. Cart contains â‰¥1 item.
3. Cart shipping section shows a current shipping address.
Steps:
--- SCREEN: Cart ---
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] Cart page loads; shipping section is visible
[ASSERT] Shipping section shows current address (any org address)
[ACT] Click the pencil/edit icon (data-test-id="select-address-button") in the shipping section
[WAIT] "Select address" dialog appears

--- SCREEN: Popup: Select address ---
[ASSERT] Dialog heading reads "Select address"
[ASSERT] Three facet dropdowns are visible: Country, State/Province, City
[ASSERT] Search field is visible and empty
[ASSERT] Address table displays â‰¥1 row with columns: Address, Description, Country, Active
[ACT] Locate the row matching @td(TECHFLOW_ADDR_US_NEW_YORK.city) in the Address column
[ACT] Click the "Select" button on that row
[WAIT] Row becomes the active row ("Select" button disappears from that row)
[ACT] Click the "OK" button
[WAIT] Dialog closes

--- SCREEN: Cart (order summary) ---
[WAIT] Cart shipping section updates (allow up to 5s for recalculation)
[ASSERT] Shipping section now shows @td(TECHFLOW_ADDR_US_NEW_YORK.city) in the address display
[ASSERT] No JS errors in browser console
Assertions:
[DOM] Dialog title == "Select address"
[DOM] After OK: shipping section city text contains @td(TECHFLOW_ADDR_US_NEW_YORK.city)
[STATE] Active row in table: "Select" button absent from selected row after click
[STATE] Dialog is closed after OK click
[FORMAT] Shipping address line format: street, city, state, country visible
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] After address change, network request to update cart/shipment resolves 2xx
[CONSOLE] No JS errors at any screen boundary
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx on cart update request
Failure_Signals:
1. Dialog does not open (pencil icon unresponsive)
2. Address table is empty despite seeded org addresses
3. "OK" click does not close dialog
4. Cart shipping section does not update after OK
5. Console shows JS error on dialog open or close
Cleanup: Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart; verify cart still shows a shipping address (any valid org address)
References: Source: VCST-4710 / PR #129; BL-CHK-003; BL-CHK-005; BL-PRICE-002

Title: Country Facet Filters Address List
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:TECHFLOW_ADMIN]
1. Authenticated B2B org user.
2. Org has addresses in â‰¥2 distinct countries (US and CA seeded).
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart

[WAIT] Dialog open; note total row count before filtering
[ACT] Click the "Country" facet dropdown
[WAIT] Country options list appears
[ACT] Select the checkbox for "United States of America" (or displayed country name)
[WAIT] Address table refreshes
Assertions:
[DOM] Country dropdown shows available countries with item counts in parentheses
[MATH] Row count after filtering < row count before filtering (if org has multi-country addresses)
[DOM] All visible rows have Country column value matching the selected country
[STATE] Country facet button shows active/selected indicator
[DOM] State/Province and City facets remain visible

[CONSOLE] No JS errors during filter application
Failure_Signals:

[WAIT] State/Province options appear

[MATH] Row count after state selection â‰¤ row count after country-only filter

Failure_Signals:
1. State/Province dropdown shows states from other countries

Title: Colombia Address Anomaly â€” Popup Does Not Show Stale/Invalid Address
Section: Checkout > Cart > Shipping Address > Data Integrity
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
1. Cart shipping section currently shows a Colombia (CO) address.
2. Popup address list does NOT include this Colombia address in org's address book.
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[ASSERT] Shipping section shows a Colombia address (observe and document the full address text)
[ACT] Open popup (click pencil icon)
[WAIT] Dialog opens
[ASSERT] Observe all rows in the address table â€” check whether the Colombia address appears
[ACT] Click Close (X) to dismiss without selection
Assertions:
[DOM] Colombia address either: (a) appears in the popup table so it CAN be managed, OR (b) is absent from the popup table (data inconsistency â€” cart holds an address not in org book)
[STATE] If absent: cart shipping address is unchanged after Close (no silent replacement)
[DOM] No error toast or crash on open when current cart address is not in popup list
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] Cart/shipment address reference vs. org address book â€” document the discrepancy
[NETWORK] No unexpected 422/500 on popup open when cart address is not in org book
Failure_Signals:
1. Popup opens and silently replaces Colombia address with a default org address (data loss â€” not user-initiated)
2. Popup crashes on open because current cart address is not in org book
3. Cart shipping address changes without user action after popup close
Cleanup: Document current state; do not change cart address
References: Source: VCST-4710 / PR #129; ECL-2.3; BL-CHK-003 â€” risk seam: cart holds address not in org book

Title: Search Field Security â€” XSS and SQL Injection Probes
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:TECHFLOW_ADMIN]
1. Authenticated B2B org user with popup open.
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart; open popup
[ACT] Type the XSS probe string (<script>alert(1)</script>) into the search field
[WAIT] 2s; check for alert dialog or console errors
[ACT] Clear the search field; type the SQL injection probe (' OR 1=1 --) into the search field
[WAIT] 2s; observe results
Assertions:
[DOM] No browser alert dialog appears (XSS not executed)
[DOM] XSS probe text displayed as literal text in search field, not rendered as HTML
[DOM] SQL injection probe returns zero results (not all records)
[CONSOLE] No JS errors triggered by probe input
[DOM] No application error page or stack trace visible
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No unhandled exceptions from probe input
[NETWORK] No 500 errors from API on probe input
Failure_Signals:
1. Alert dialog appears (XSS executed â€” critical vulnerability)
2. Search returns ALL addresses on SQLi probe (injection successful)
3. Application error page shown on probe input
References: Source: VCST-4710 / PR #129; Error Guessing; OWASP A03

[DOM] Address table contains ONLY personal addresses belonging to {{USER2_EMAIL}} â€” no org addresses from any other account visible

Title: Personal User â€” Popup Column Schema Differs from B2B (Recipient Name/Phone/Email vs Description/Country)
Preconditions:

Title: State/Province Facet Visible but Shows Only US States When Org Has Mixed US + GB Addresses
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:TECHFLOW_ADMIN]

[ACT] Open popup (no facets applied)
[WAIT] Dialog opens with full unfiltered org address list

[ACT] Click "State/Province" facet dropdown
[WAIT] Options list appears
[ASSERT] Check whether GB/UK region values appear alongside US state values
[ACT] Verify GB address rows are still visible in the unfiltered table
Assertions:
[DOM] State/Province facet IS present (US addresses drive term aggregation for regionId)
[DOM] Dropdown options contain only US state values â€” no GB region/county entries (GB addresses have null regionId â†’ not indexed in aggregation)
[DOM] GB address rows (e.g., @td(TECHFLOW_ADDR_UK_LONDON.city)) are visible in the unfiltered address table
[MATH] GB rows are NOT hidden or excluded from the list merely because State/Province facet is active in unselected state

3. GB addresses disappear from table when State/Province facet is opened (unintended filter side-effect)
References: Source: VCST-4710 / PR #129; BL-CHK-008; Error Guessing / Classification Tree (mixed regionId population â€” some null, some non-null).
